title: Make getting-started reach first success
description: Design the getting-started path so a new reader goes from zero to a working result with copyable, verified steps and no hidden prerequisites.
body: Use when writing or revising onboarding/quickstart docs. Do not assume unstated prerequisites, skip setup, or leave steps that only work in the author's environment. Actions: state prerequisites up front, give copy-paste commands in order, show expected output at each step, and end at a concrete working result. Validation signals include running the quickstart from a clean environment and reaching the stated success state. Risk boundary: a quickstart that fails on a clean machine erodes trust immediately; verify it end-to-end from scratch rather than trusting that the steps look right.
